sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I extended the path from the player's position (rows 18-23), the red block moved to the end of the path! The red block is now at the right end of the path.
So the mechanic is:
1. Move the player to the row position of the block you want to move.
2. Extend the path to the right.
3. The block moves to the end of the path.
4. Retract the path (move LEFT).
5. The block stays at the new position.
So I can use this to rearrange the blocks! The current order is: red (top), blue (middle), green (bottom). The desired order is: red (top), green (middle), blue (bottom).
So I need to:
1. Keep red at rows 19-22 (already correct)
2. Move green to rows 25-28 (currently blue is here)
3. Move blue to rows 31-34 (currently green is here)
To do this:
1. First, move the player to the green block's position (rows 31-34).
2. Extend the path to the right, which will move the green block to the end of the path.
3. Retract the path.
4. The green block is now at the right end.
5. Move the player to the blue block's position (rows 25-28).
6. Extend the path to the right, which will move the blue block to the end of the path (overlapping with the green block's new position).
7. Retract the path.
Wait, this doesn't work because the blocks would overlap.
Let me think about this differently. The blocks are placed at the player's row position when the path is extended. So:
- If I extend the path from rows 18-23 (player's current position), the red block moves to the end of the path.
- If I extend the path from rows 25-28, the blue block would move to the end of the path.
- If I extend the path from rows 31-34, the green block would move to the end of the path.
So the blocks are placed at the player's row position when the path is extended. This means:
- To place a block at rows 19-22, the player must be at rows 18-23.
- To place a block at rows 25-28, the player must be at rows 24-29.
- To place a block at rows 31-34, the player must be at rows 30-35.
Currently, the red block is at the end of the path (right side). Let me retract the path first.
